violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Establishment has employees certified in an approved food safety management course. One of these employees must register with RIDOH Center for Food Protection as the Certified Food Safety Manager. Application provided. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026
Container was observed in the kitchen hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2026

Deli meats observed not marked with the date of preparation (opening package). Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Cooked chicken was observed cooling in wrapped individual serving packages in the prep unit. Chicken had been cooked this morning and was observed at 56 degrees F. Chicken packages were untied and spread out on sheet pans to complete the cooling process. Cooling methods reviewed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2026

Working container of salt observed not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2026

The door to the outside, located at the back of the establishment, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2026

Utensils (forks, knives) are displayed with the lip surface up. Utensils are to be stored so that only the handles are touched by employees and consumers if consumer self-service is provided. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2026

The following observed in poor repair: Gaskets in a couple of prep units have split away from the doors and must be replaced. Can opener is heavily rusted and must be replaced. Door to the walk-in cooler, condenser fan guard in the walk-in freezer, and top cover of a prep unit are broken and must be repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 03/18/2026

Individual cutting boards are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quat sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink. Test strips must be obtained. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the shelving throughout the establishment and in refrigeration units, as well as the condenser fan guards in the walk-in cooler, have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. These areas must be cleaned. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

Walls throughout the facility and floor under the cook/grill/fryolator line have an accumulation of soil residue, grease, and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils can opener were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Can opener was moved to 3-bay sink at the time of the inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2025

Deli meat and cheese was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Dates were added to the food at the time of the inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2025

The door to the outside, located in the back of the store, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 01/31/2025
The fan guard located in the walk-in freezer was not in good repair. Equipment must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quat sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink. Establishment is to obtain test strips. New Violation. Correct By: 01/16/2025

The following areas have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. -Wall behind the grill line -Walk-in cooler ceiling -The floors throughout the kitchen The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 01/10/2025
Multiple ceiling tiles in the kitchen area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2025
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2023

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2023
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink New Violation. Correct By: 06/14/2023
The non-food contact surfaces under the tables in the kitchen have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2023

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2023

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the kitchen. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, vegetable slicer knives) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2023

The Walk in Cooler was observed held at 59 degrees Fahrenheit. Misc. food items were observed at between 55-60 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. CA - Misc food items were voluntarily disposed of. Establishment is to use walk in freezer of stand alone units until Walk in is fixed. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2023

The establishment is lacking a thermometer to test food temperatures. Food temperature measuring devices shall be provided and be readily accessible for checking cooking, cooling, reheating, and hot/cold holding temperatures of time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Thermometers are lacking from refrigeration units. Thermometers must be present in all refrigeration units. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2023

The door to the outside, located in the back, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the prep units have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. The Non-food contact surfaces of the kitchen were observed with an accumulation of dust, dirt and food residue. Establishment must clean maintain non food contact surfaces. New Violation. Correct By: 06/06/2023
